Cruz-Calayan Subd Project, Guavaville Subd , St. Claire Subd., Golden Roseville , Isarog Heights Subd 1 & 2, e, Balconville, Sto. Nino Village, Robredo Village, Rioville Subd., Nagayon Subdivision etc. and all other on-site and off-site projects of the city.
items.output_indicators: - Number of housing project sites with completed partial site development works. - Length (meters) of internal roads constructed or concreted within the housing sites. - Length (meters) of drainage lines installed or improved. - Number of water system components installed (e.g., pipelines, communal faucets, service connections). - Number of electrical system installations completed (e.g., poles, wiring, streetlights). - Number of community support facilities constructed or improved within the sites. - Percentage of planned site development components completed per project site.
items.outcome_indicators: - Percentage of households in Liberty Village I & II and St. Isidore with access to basic utilities (water, electricity, road access, drainage). - Reduction in flooding or drainage-related issues within the housing sites. - Improved mobility and accessibility within the housing communities due to developed road networks. - Increased occupancy or readiness of housing units for beneficiary families. - Beneficiary satisfaction rate with the availability and quality of basic site infrastructure.

items.description: Facilitate bidding for subdivision survey approval of KSK project sites (Nagayon Village, Uswag Balatas, Balconville, Liberty Village 2, St. Isidore, Palmera 2 & 3, Maogma Village, St. Claire Subd, Capilihan Subd., Isarog Heights Subd 2, Acasia-Abella Subd., Guavaville, Immaculate Village, Upville, Mabolo-Calzada, Tabuco-Lees Sing Giap, etc.
items.output_indicators: - Number of project sites included in the bidding process for subdivision survey approval. - Bidding documents prepared and published (e.g., Terms of Reference, technical specifications, cost estimates). - Number of procurement activities completed (pre-bid conference, bid evaluation, post-qualification). - Number of contracts awarded to qualified survey service providers. - Percentage of targeted KSK project sites with awarded survey contracts. - Procurement cycle time from posting to contract award (days).
items.outcome_indicators: - Percentage of KSK project sites with completed subdivision surveys submitted for approval. - Number of project sites progressing to subdivision plan approval and titling/land development stage. - Reduction in processing time for subdivision survey approval of housing projects. -Increased readiness of housing sites for CMP, socialized housing development, or land disposition to beneficiaries. - Improved compliance of subdivision plans with technical and regulatory requirements.

programs.name: Informal Settler Monitoring and Housing Program

items.description: Continual Field verification and Monitoring of Informal Settlers as to the number, location of settlements ,living conditions and land tenure occupancy in areas under the program and also outside the coverage area of the program
items.output_indicators: - Number of field verification and monitoring activities conducted (quarterly/annual). - Number of informal settler families profiled and validated. - Percentage of ISF data updated in the LGU housing database. - Number of settlement areas mapped and geo-tagged. - Number of monitoring reports submitted and endorsed for planning/action. - Existence of updated master list of ISFs disaggregated by location and tenure status.
items.outcome_indicators: - Reduction in discrepancies between estimated and validated ISF data. - Increased percentage of housing interventions (resettlement, CMP, material assistance, lot allocation) based on validated field data. - Improved response time to emerging informal settlements. - Reduction in growth rate of new informal settlement areas due to early monitoring and intervention. - Increased proportion of identified ISFs enrolled in appropriate housing or tenure security programs.

programs.name: KSK Program Property Management and Legalization

items.description: Registration and transfer of ownership of properties acquired for KSK Program to the city
items.output_indicators: - Number of properties formally registered under the City of Naga City for KSK Program. - Percentage of acquired properties with completed title transfer. - Number of transfer documents (Deeds of Sale, Transfer Certificates of Title, etc.) executed and notarized. - Average processing time from property acquisition to registration. - Number of properties with updated LGU land records.
items.outcome_indicators: - Percentage of KSK properties legally owned and managed by the city. - Increased capacity of the city to implement housing projects using acquired properties. - Reduction in disputes over property ownership within the KSK Program. - Number of beneficiary households able to access housing due to secure property ownership. - Improved efficiency in subsequent development or distribution of housing projects.

programs.name: Miscellaneous Infrastructure Development and Flood Control Program

items.description: Other Various Infra Assets ( Roads/Flood Control)
items.output_indicators: - Total length of roads constructed, rehabilitated, or improved (in kilometers or meters). - Total area of roads paved or concreted (in square meters). - Number of flood control structures constructed or rehabilitated (e.g., drainage lines, box culverts, revetments). - Linear meters of drainage or flood mitigation structures completed. - Percentage of infrastructure projects completed within approved timeline and budget. - Number of barangays/areas served by completed infrastructure assets.
items.outcome_indicators: - Reduction in average travel time in improved road sections. - Reduction in flood incidence, flood depth, or duration in affected areas. - Number of households/businesses benefiting from improved road access and flood protection. - Decrease in reported infrastructure-related complaints (road damage, flooding). - Increased accessibility of emergency and service vehicles during adverse weather conditions. - Improvement in road condition rating (from poor/fair to good condition).

programs.name: Public Buildings and Vertical Infrastructure Development Program

items.description: Repair of HSDO Office (leaking rooftop and construction of drainage system beside the office
items.output_indicators: Roofing system repaired/replaced and restored to watertight condition
items.outcome_indicators: - Reduce weather-related service disruptions to zero during the rainy season - Eliminate 100% of reported roof leak incidents within six months after repair completion

items.description: Resettlement lot distribution Program to beneficiaries who are in need of land for housing.
items.output_indicators: - Number of resettlement lots allocated and awarded. - Total land area (in square meters/hectares) distributed to beneficiaries. - Number of beneficiary families issued with Certificates of Lot Award (CLA) or equivalent tenure documents. - Percentage of identified qualified beneficiaries served under the program. - Number of orientation and validation activities conducted prior to lot distribution.
items.outcome_indicators: - Percentage of beneficiary families with secure tenure over awarded lots. - Reduction in the number of informal settler families in high-risk or non-tenured areas. - Increase in the proportion of low-income households with legally recognized residential lots. - Beneficiary occupancy rate of distributed resettlement lots. - Reduction in housing backlog attributable to land tenure interventions.

items.description: Issuance of Contract to Sell, or Usufruct Agreement or Deed of Sale to beneficiaries of the program
items.output_indicators: - Number of Contracts to Sell issued. - Number of Usufruct Agreements executed. - Number of Deeds of Sale executed and notarized. - Percentage of qualified beneficiaries issued with appropriate tenure documents. - Average processing time from beneficiary qualification to issuance of tenure instrument. - Number of signed and recorded agreements properly filed in LGU records.
items.outcome_indicators: - Percentage of beneficiary households with legally recognized tenure documents. - Reduction in tenure insecurity among program beneficiaries. - Increase in compliance rate with amortization or usufruct terms (if applicable). - Decrease in land-related disputes within housing/resettlement sites. - Increase in eligibility of beneficiaries to access utilities, financing, or home improvement assistance due to formalized tenure.

items.description: Distribution of Land Titles to Fully Paid beneficiaries
items.output_indicators: - Number of land titles prepared, notarized, and ready for distribution. - Number of fully paid beneficiaries who received their land titles. - Percentage of fully paid beneficiaries issued with land titles within the target period. - Average processing time from payment completion to title issuance. - Number of land titles recorded in LGU/property registry
items.outcome_indicators: - Percentage of beneficiaries with legally recognized property ownership. - Reduction in disputes or claims over awarded lots. - Increased satisfaction among beneficiaries regarding land tenure security. - Number of beneficiaries able to use land titles for legal, financial, or improvement purposes (e.g., accessing loans or housing improvements). - Strengthened compliance with LGU housing programs and housing backlog reduction targets.

programs.name: Socialized Housing Program Support and Development

items.description: Crafting of support mechanisms and interventions for the socialized housing program of the city
items.output_indicators: - Number of policy guidelines, resolutions, or ordinances drafted and endorsed. - Number of support programs designed (e.g., financing schemes, livelihood linkage, material assistance, tenure facilitation). - Number of stakeholder consultations, technical working group (TWG) meetings, or workshops conducted. - Completion of program framework or implementation manual. - Number of partnership agreements initiated (e.g., with national agencies, NGOs, private developers). - Establishment of monitoring and evaluation (M&E) system for housing interventions.
items.outcome_indicators: - Increased percentage of housing projects implemented with structured support mechanisms. - Reduction in processing time for housing-related applications and approvals. - Increased beneficiary access to financing, tenure instruments, or site development assistance. - Improved coordination among LGU offices and external partners involved in housing. - Measurable reduction in housing backlog attributable to strengthened program mechanisms. - Increased beneficiary satisfaction with housing program services.

items.description: Housing Material Assistance to urban poor beneficiaries
items.output_indicators: - Number of beneficiary households provided with housing material assistance. - Total value of housing materials distributed (₱). - Type and quantity of materials distributed (e.g., roofing sheets, lumber, cement, hollow blocks). - Percentage of identified qualified beneficiaries served. - Number of distribution activities conducted. - Percentage of beneficiaries who utilized the materials within the prescribed period.
items.outcome_indicators: - Percentage of assisted households reporting improved housing condition (roofing, walls, flooring). - Reduction in number of structurally unsafe or substandard housing units among beneficiaries. - Increased resilience of beneficiary homes to weather-related risks (e.g., reduced roof leakage incidents). - Improvement in beneficiary satisfaction with housing condition. - Contribution to reduction in informal or makeshift housing within the city.

programs.name: Urban Poor Support and Livelihood Program

items.description: NAGA CITY URBAN POOR FEDERATION FUND
items.output_indicators: - Total amount of funds allocated and released (₱). - Number of federation-affiliated communities assisted. - Number of projects or activities funded (e.g., CMP processing, savings mobilization, community mapping, capacity building). - Number of beneficiaries directly supported by funded activities. - Percentage of fund utilization within the fiscal year. - Number of financial and accomplishment reports submitted by the federation.
items.outcome_indicators: - Increased number of urban poor communities actively participating in city housing programs. - Increased savings mobilization or equity build-up among federation members (if applicable). - Number/percentage of federation-led projects progressing to implementation stage (e.g., CMP enrolment, site development). - Improved access of federation members to housing, tenure security, or social services. - Enhanced collaboration between the LGU and organized urban poor groups in policy and program implementation.

items.outcome_indicators: - Percentage of operational uptime of the facility annually. - Number of youth participants utilizing the hub per year. - User satisfaction rate regarding facility condition and functionality. - Increase in youth-led programs, trainings, or innovation activities conducted at the hub. - Reduced downtime or service interruptions due to facility-related issues.
